<user_query>
I am a student in Hong Kong, I am studying Physics. However, I struggle with the circuits. Therefore, now I want you to build me a website where it can show a circuit diagram of what that I upload( like a photo, a circuit diagram or just by words)
</user_query>

Exploring your workspace and clarifying a few requirements so we can plan a circuit-diagram website that fits how you'll use it.

[REDACTED]

questions.prompt: How should the website interpret your input? (This affects complexity and whether you need API keys)

options.label: AI-powered: photo + text → auto-detect components and draw the circuit (needs OpenAI/similar API key)
Text/description only: you describe the circuit in words and it draws it (simpler, can use rules or light AI)
Interactive builder: drag-and-drop resistors, batteries, etc. (no photo upload, but easiest to learn)
Hybrid: text/photo upload with AI + manual editing of the diagram after

questions.prompt: Beyond showing the diagram, what else do you need?

options.label: Component labels and values (R1=10Ω, V=9V, etc.)
Basic circuit solving (total resistance, current, voltage drops)
Step-by-step explanations in plain language (good for studying)
Just the diagram — keep it simple

content.text:
<user_query>
moreover, I want you to show the original codes on te website so developers can edit and debug more easily. Furthermore, I want you to show the flow of electrics, current, resistace, voltage and other things that you can think of in the website
</user_query>

Incorporating editable source code and live electrical flow into the plan. One quick clarification will shape the architecture.

[REDACTED]

options.label: Circuit definition code — editable text/JSON that describes the circuit (e.g. components, connections); changing it updates the diagram
Full app source code — show the website’s own HTML/JS/Python files in an editor on the page
Both — circuit definition editor plus a “view app source” section for developers

questions.prompt: When you say “show the original codes on the website”, what do you mean?

options.label: Yes — I can provide an API key (OpenAI, Google Gemini, etc.) for photo/diagram recognition
Start without AI — text description + manual editing first; add photo upload later
No paid API — use only free/local tools (more limited photo recognition)

questions.prompt: Photo/diagram upload needs AI or OCR to interpret. Which approach works for you?

Designing the architecture with editable circuit-definition code, AI-powered input parsing, and live electrical-flow visualization.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Circuit Visualizer Website
input.overview: Build a React-based circuit learning website where students upload a photo, diagram, or text description; an AI backend converts input into editable circuit-definition JSON; a live diagram and physics panel show current flow, voltage, and resistance with bidirectional code↔diagram sync.
input.plan:
# Circuit Visualizer Website

## Goal

A local web app for HK Physics students that turns **photos**, **diagram images**, or **text descriptions** into an **interactive circuit diagram**, with an **editable circuit-definition code panel** and **animated electrical flow** (current, voltage, resistance, power).

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph inputs [Input]
    Photo[Photo upload]
    Image[Circuit diagram image]
    Text[Text description]
    Code[Circuit JSON editor]
  end

  subgraph backend [Backend API]
    Vision[Vision LLM parser]
    TextLLM[Text LLM parser]
    Solver[DC circuit solver]
  end

  subgraph frontend [Frontend]
    Editor[Monaco code editor]
    Canvas[SVG circuit canvas]
    Physics[Physics panel]
  end

  Photo --> Vision
  Image --> Vision
  Text --> TextLLM
  Vision --> Editor
  TextLLM --> Editor
  Code --> Editor
  Editor --> Canvas
  Editor --> Solver
  Solver --> Physics
  Canvas --> Physics
  Canvas -->|drag/edit components| Editor
```

## Project layout

Create a new project at [`c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer):

```
circuit-visualizer/
  frontend/          # React + Vite + TypeScript
  backend/           # Node.js + Express (or Python FastAPI — Node keeps one language)
  shared/            # Circuit JSON schema + solver (shared types)
  .env.example       # OPENAI_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY
  README.md          # Setup + example circuits for students
```

## Circuit definition format (the “code” on the website)

Human-readable JSON that students and developers can edit directly. Example:

```json
{
  "name": "Series resistors",
  "components": [
    { "id": "V1", "type": "voltage_source", "value": 12, "unit": "V", "nodes": ["n0", "n1"] },
    { "id": "R1", "type": "resistor", "value": 4, "unit": "ohm", "nodes": ["n1", "n2"] },
    { "id": "R2", "type": "resistor", "value": 8, "unit": "ohm", "nodes": ["n2", "n0"] }
  ],
  "layout": {
    "V1": { "x": 100, "y": 200 },
    "R1": { "x": 250, "y": 200 },
    "R2": { "x": 400, "y": 200 }
  }
}
```

Supported component types for **v1** (HK secondary Physics scope):

| Type | Symbol | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| `voltage_source` | Battery | DC only |
| `resistor` | Zigzag | Value in Ω |
| `wire` | Line | Zero resistance |
| `switch` | Open/closed | Boolean `closed` |
| `ammeter` | Circle A | Shows branch current |
| `voltmeter` | Circle V | Shows node voltage diff |
| `bulb` | Lamp | R = V²/P or fixed R |

**Bidirectional sync:** editing JSON re-renders the diagram; dragging components on canvas updates `layout` in JSON; adding/removing components updates `components` array.

## Input methods

### 1. Text description
- User types: *"12V battery in series with 4Ω and 8Ω resistors"*
- Backend sends text + JSON schema to LLM → returns validated circuit JSON
- Show parsing confidence; let user fix in code editor if AI misreads

### 2. Photo / diagram image
- User uploads JPG/PNG (phone photo of textbook, hand-drawn sketch, or printed diagram)
- Backend sends image to **vision-capable model** (OpenAI GPT-4o or Google Gemini) with a strict system prompt: output only circuit JSON matching our schema
- Post-process: validate JSON, flag unknown symbols, default layout if missing

### 3. Manual code editor
- [Monaco Editor](https://microsoft.github.io/monaco-editor/) embedded in left/bottom panel
- Syntax validation on blur; inline errors (e.g. dangling node, duplicate id)
- “Reset example” buttons for common HK syllabus circuits (series, parallel, mixed)

**API key handling:** stored in `backend/.env` only; never sent to browser. Frontend calls `POST /api/parse-text` and `POST /api/parse-image`.

## Electrical flow visualization

After the solver runs nodal analysis (DC, linear components):

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Solve[Nodal analysis] --> Branch[Per-branch current]
  Solve --> Node[Per-node voltage]
  Branch --> Anim[Animated current arrows]
  Branch --> Labels[Component labels]
  Node --> VoltDrop[Voltage drop badges]
  Branch --> Power[Power P=IV heat tint]
```

**On the SVG canvas:**
- **Current flow:** animated dots/arrows along wires; speed ∝ |I| (capped for readability); direction = conventional current (+ → −)
- **Voltage:** color gradient or badges on each component (e.g. `ΔV = 4.0 V`)
- **Resistance:** label on each resistor (`R = 4 Ω`)
- **Totals panel:** equivalent resistance, total current, power per component
- **Study mode toggle:** step-by-step explanation in plain English (e.g. *"R₁ and R₂ are in series, so R_eq = 4 + 8 = 12 Ω"*) generated from solver results + optional LLM polish

**Interactive controls:**
- Sliders to change V or R values → instant re-solve + animation update
- Switch open/closed toggle
- Pause/play animation

**Solver scope (v1):** single DC source, resistors, switches; no capacitors/inductors/transistors yet (can extend schema later).

## UI layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [Upload Photo] [Upload Diagram]  │  Text: _____________  │
│                          [Generate Circuit]                 │
├──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Circuit Code (JSON) │  Circuit Diagram (SVG + animations)  │
│  Monaco Editor       │  Zoom, pan, component drag           │
│  [Validate] [Format] │  Current / voltage overlays           │
├──────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Physics Results: I_total, R_eq, V drops, P, explanations  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Clean, student-friendly styling (large labels, SI units, optional Traditional Chinese labels later).

## Tech stack

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|-------|--------|-----|
| Frontend | React 18 + Vite + TypeScript | Fast dev, component model |
| Diagram | Custom SVG renderer | Full control over animations |
| Code editor | `@monaco-editor/react` | Familiar VS Code editing |
| Backend | Express + multer (image upload) | Simple API proxy for LLM |
| AI | OpenAI or Gemini (user picks via env) | Photo + text parsing |
| Solver | Custom nodal analysis in `shared/solver.ts` | No heavy deps, testable |
| Validation | Zod schema for circuit JSON | Safe AI output parsing |

## Key files to implement

- [`shared/schema.ts`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\shared\schema.ts) — Zod circuit schema + types
- [`shared/solver.ts`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\shared\solver.ts) — DC nodal analysis, branch currents
- [`backend/src/routes/parse.ts`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\backend\src\routes\parse.ts) — `/api/parse-text`, `/api/parse-image`
- [`backend/src/llm/prompts.ts`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\backend\src\llm\prompts.ts) — Schema-constrained prompts
- [`frontend/src/components/CircuitCanvas.tsx`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\frontend\src\components\CircuitCanvas.tsx) — SVG symbols + current animation
- [`frontend/src/components/CodePanel.tsx`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\frontend\src\components\CodePanel.tsx) — Monaco + validation
- [`frontend/src/components/PhysicsPanel.tsx`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\frontend\src\components\PhysicsPanel.tsx) — numbers + study explanations
- [`frontend/src/hooks/useCircuitSync.ts`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\frontend\src\hooks\useCircuitSync.ts) — code ↔ diagram state

## AI prompt strategy (reliability)

Vision/text prompts will:
1. Require output as **only** JSON matching our schema
2. List allowed component types and node naming rules
3. Ask for best-guess `layout` coordinates if not inferable
4. Return `warnings[]` for ambiguous symbols (e.g. unclear parallel vs series)

Backend validates with Zod; if invalid, retry once with error feedback to LLM.

## Setup for you

1. `npm install` in `frontend/` and `backend/`
2. Copy `.env.example` → `backend/.env`, add `OPENAI_API_KEY=...` or `GEMINI_API_KEY=...`
3. `npm run dev` (concurrently starts frontend :5173 + backend :3001)
4. Open browser → upload textbook photo or type description → edit JSON → watch current flow

## Out of scope for v1 (future)

- AC circuits, capacitors, inductors
- Full SPICE netlist import
- Traditional Chinese UI (easy to add i18n later)
- User accounts / cloud save
- Mobile-native app (responsive web is enough)

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| AI misreads hand-drawn circuits | Editable JSON + warnings; manual fix is first-class |
| Complex non-planar layouts | Auto-layout heuristic + manual drag |
| Invalid circuits (short circuit) | Solver detects and shows clear error |
| API cost | Image parsing only on button click; cache last result locally |

OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-your-key-here
OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini
PORT=3001

# Circuit Visualizer

A Physics study tool for Hong Kong secondary students. Upload a photo, describe a circuit in words, or edit the circuit JSON directly — then watch **current flow**, **voltage**, and **resistance** update in real time.

## Features

- **Photo / diagram upload** — AI parses textbook photos or hand-drawn sketches into circuit JSON
- **Text description** — e.g. *"12V battery with 4Ω and 8Ω in series"*
- **Editable circuit code** — Monaco JSON editor with bidirectional sync to the diagram
- **Animated current flow** — conventional current direction and speed
- **Physics panel** — total current, R_eq, power, step-by-step study notes
- **Interactive sliders** — change V or R and see instant updates

## Prerequisites

- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) 18 or newer
- OpenAI API key (for photo/text parsing)

## Setup

```bash
cd circuit-visualizer
npm install
npm run build -w shared
cp .env.example backend/.env
# Edit backend/.env and add your OPENAI_API_KEY
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173) in your browser.

## Project structure

```
circuit-visualizer/
  frontend/     React + Vite UI
  backend/      Express API (AI parsing proxy)
  shared/       Circuit schema + DC solver
```

## Circuit JSON format

```json
{
  "name": "Series resistors",
  "components": [
    { "id": "V1", "type": "voltage_source", "value": 12, "unit": "V", "nodes": ["n0", "n1"] },
    { "id": "R1", "type": "resistor", "value": 4, "unit": "ohm", "nodes": ["n1", "n2"] },
    { "id": "R2", "type": "resistor", "value": 8, "unit": "ohm", "nodes": ["n2", "n0"] }
  ],
  "layout": {
    "V1": { "x": 80, "y": 200 },
    "R1": { "x": 280, "y": 200 },
    "R2": { "x": 480, "y": 200 }
  }
}
```

Supported types: `voltage_source`, `resistor`, `wire`, `switch`, `ammeter`, `voltmeter`, `bulb`.

## Keyboard shortcuts

- **Ctrl+S** — Apply JSON changes to the diagram

## Tips for students

1. Start with an **Example** (Series / Parallel / Mixed) to see how the JSON maps to the diagram.
2. If AI misreads your photo, fix the JSON in the code panel — that's expected for messy sketches.
3. Double-click a **switch** to open/close it.
4. Drag components to rearrange the layout.

## API endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/health` | Health check |
| POST | `/api/parse-text` | `{ "text": "..." }` → circuit JSON |
| POST | `/api/parse-image` | multipart `image` → circuit JSON |

## License

MIT — for educational use.

content.text:
The **Circuit Visualizer** is implemented at `c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer`. All plan todos are complete.

## What was built

### Input methods
- **Photo/diagram upload** — sends image to AI (OpenAI or Gemini)
- **Text description** — e.g. *"12V battery with 4Ω and 8Ω in series"*
- **Editable JSON code** — Monaco editor with Apply (Ctrl+S) and Format

### Circuit diagram & physics
- SVG symbols: battery, resistor, switch, ammeter, voltmeter, bulb, wire
- **Animated current flow** (speed ∝ current, conventional direction)
- **Voltage / current / resistance labels** on components
- **Power heat tint** on components
- **Zoom** (scroll or +/-) and **pan** (Alt+drag)
- Drag components to update layout in JSON
- Double-click switches to toggle open/closed

### Physics panel
- Total current, equivalent resistance, power per component
- Sliders to change V and R live
- Step-by-step study notes (series, parallel, KVL-style explanations)

### Examples
Series, Parallel, Mixed, and Switch circuits (one-click load)

## Project structure

```
circuit-visualizer/
  frontend/     React + Vite + Monaco editor
  backend/      Express API (AI parsing)
  shared/       Zod schema + DC nodal solver
  README.md
  .env.example
```

## How to run

Node.js is not installed on your machine yet (install failed without admin approval). Install it first:

1. Download [Node.js LTS](https://nodejs.org/) and install (or run the installer as Administrator)
2. Then in a terminal:

```bash
cd c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer
npm install
npm run build -w shared
copy .env.example backend\.env
```

3. Edit `backend\.env` and add your API key:

```
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-your-key-here
# OR
GEMINI_API_KEY=your-gemini-key
AI_PROVIDER=openai   # or gemini
```

4. Start the app:

```bash
npm run dev
```

5. Open **http://localhost:5173**

## Quick test without AI

Click **Series**, **Parallel**, or **Mixed** — the diagram, current animation, and physics panel work without an API key. AI upload/text parsing needs a key in `backend\.env`.

If you want, I can help you install Node.js or walk through your first circuit once it’s running.

input.contents:
⚡ Circuit Visualizer

Physics study tool — paste circuit JSON from Poe, edit code, watch current flow

Tip: Ask any Poe bot to output circuit JSON (use prompt in POE_BOT_PROMPT.txt), then paste below and click Apply.

Circuit code (JSON)

Diagram

100% Scroll on diagram to zoom

Physics results

# Run Circuit Visualizer on Poe

You have **two easy options** — no Node.js or server needed.

---

## Option A: Poe Canvas App (recommended — interactive diagram in Poe)

1. Go to [poe.com](https://poe.com) and sign in.
2. Open **Create** → **Canvas** (or **App** / **Create app** — name may vary).
3. Create a new Canvas app.
4. Open the file **`circuit-visualizer.html`** from the `poe` folder in this project.
5. **Copy the entire file** (Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C) and paste it into the Canvas HTML editor.
6. Save / Publish the app.
7. Open your app — you get the full interactive circuit visualizer inside Poe.

### How to use it
- Click **Series**, **Parallel**, or **Mixed** to load examples.
- Type a description like `12V with 4 ohm and 8 ohm in series` and click **Quick parse**.
- Or chat with a Poe bot (see Option B), copy the JSON it gives you, paste into the code box, click **Apply**.

---

## Option B: Pair with a Poe bot (for photos & complex circuits)

### Step 1 — Create a helper bot on Poe
1. Poe → **Create bot**.
2. Name it e.g. **Circuit JSON Maker**.
3. Paste the full text from **`POE_BOT_PROMPT.txt`** as the bot’s **system prompt / instructions**.
4. Save the bot.

### Step 2 — Get circuit JSON from the bot
- Describe your circuit: *"9V battery, 6 ohm and 3 ohm resistors in parallel"*
- Or describe a photo: *"Textbook diagram: 12V battery, 4 ohm resistor in series with 8 ohm"*
- The bot replies with JSON only.

### Step 3 — Visualize
- Open your **Canvas app** from Option A (or open `circuit-visualizer.html` in any browser).
- Paste the JSON into the **Circuit code** box.
- Click **Apply** → diagram, current flow, voltage, and study notes appear.

---

## Option C: Open in browser (no Poe)

Double-click **`poe/circuit-visualizer.html`** or drag it into Chrome/Edge. Works offline.

---

## What works without extra setup

| Feature | Works? |
|---------|--------|
| Example circuits | Yes |
| Edit JSON code | Yes |
| Animated current flow | Yes |
| Voltage / current / power | Yes |
| Sliders & switches | Yes |
| Quick text parse (simple series/parallel) | Yes |
| Photo upload AI | Use Poe bot (Option B) → paste JSON |

---

## Files in this folder

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `circuit-visualizer.html` | **Paste this into Poe Canvas** — complete app in one file |
| `POE_BOT_PROMPT.txt` | Paste into a Poe bot for AI circuit → JSON |
| `HOW_TO_USE_ON_POE.md` | This guide |

content.text:
I've turned the project into a **single file** you can use on Poe — no Node.js or server needed.

## Files created

| File | What to do with it |
|------|-------------------|
| [`poe/circuit-visualizer.html`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\poe\circuit-visualizer.html) | **Copy the whole file** into Poe Canvas |
| [`poe/POE_BOT_PROMPT.txt`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\poe\POE_BOT_PROMPT.txt) | Paste into a Poe bot for AI → JSON |
| [`poe/HOW_TO_USE_ON_POE.md`](c:\Users\2c30\circuit-visualizer\poe\HOW_TO_USE_ON_POE.md) | Step-by-step guide |

---

## Quick start on Poe

### 1. Interactive app (Canvas)
1. Go to [poe.com](https://poe.com) → **Create** → **Canvas** (or **App**)
2. Open `poe/circuit-visualizer.html` on your PC
3. **Ctrl+A → Ctrl+C** (copy everything)
4. Paste into the Canvas HTML editor → **Save / Publish**
5. Open the app — you get the full visualizer inside Poe

### 2. AI for photos & complex circuits (optional)
1. Create a new Poe bot
2. Paste the contents of `POE_BOT_PROMPT.txt` as the bot instructions
3. Ask it: *"12V battery with 4Ω and 8Ω in series"*
4. Copy the JSON it returns → paste into the app → click **Apply**

---

## What’s included in the single HTML file

- Circuit JSON editor (code panel)
- Animated current flow, voltage, resistance, power
- Series / Parallel / Mixed examples
- Simple text parser (“12V with 4 ohm and 8 ohm in series”)
- Sliders, switches, drag components, zoom

---

## No Poe? Use a browser

Double-click `poe/circuit-visualizer.html` — it works offline in Chrome or Edge.

Photo upload AI isn’t built into the HTML file (Poe can’t run a backend). Use the Poe bot for that, then paste the JSON into the app.
